mnemonics: CONNECTED: Can Only Navigate Nice Edges; No Terrible Disconnections Everywhere Daily (to remember that a graph is connected if there is a path between every pair of vertices)
keyPoints: A graph is connected if there is a path between every pair of vertices.
A disconnected graph is made up of two or more connected components.
The number of edges needed to connect a graph with n vertices is at least n-1 (a tree).
commonMistakes: Assuming that if a graph has many edges, it must be connected.
Confusing a graph having an Eulerian trail (which requires exactly 0 or 2 vertices of odd degree) with it being connected (a fundamental requirement for an Eulerian trail).
Thinking that a graph with a cycle is automatically connected.
